text:
Market today: Rough tape for your book. Tech had one of its worst weeks in a year - AI momentum is cracking, chip names are under real pressure, and now a U.S. strike on Iran is pulling oil up in after-hours, which adds macro uncertainty on top of an already fragile setup. Your sector is the epicenter.

Your holdings: Three need attention. AVGO is your biggest position, down 16% and flashing SELL - if it keeps sliding toward that $336 stop you're taking a serious hit, amplified by margin. PLTR is down 13% and also on SELL - high-beta momentum name in a week when momentum got crushed. SPAL is down nearly 17% - this is a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily, and SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no track record; both are dangerous to hold passively on margin.

Watch: 1) Oil prices after the Iran strike - escalation spooks risk assets fast. 2) Whether chip names stabilize or Micron's 6% drop this week signals more sector selling ahead.

Bottom line: Your two biggest losers are screaming for action - margin means you can't afford to let AVGO and PLTR drift to their stops.
